thesis: Closed-loop adaptive systems for human state + context variation
problemStatement: Human perception, biomechanics, and cognition change across people and situations. Fixed XR/vehicle/wearable systems can reduce safety, comfort, and performance.
approachStatement: Sense user+context → infer state/intent → adapt multimodal feedback/control → optimize metrics (safety, comfort, performance, presence, trust).
handoff1to2: Perception + attention limits bound what actuation/feedback is effective and comfortable.
handoff2to3: Motor-level sensing/actuation enables task adaptation; tasks constrain timing, complexity, and safety.

layers.name: SENSORY MODELS
layers.goal: Model multisensory perception + attention; expand usable envelope without discomfort.
systemAdapted: Gait-based locomotion prediction (robust to visual distraction)
layers.keyImpact: More reliable XR locomotion/reorientation and improved accessibility with preserved presence/comfort.

layers.name: MOTOR MODELS
layers.goal: Infer physical intent and deliver biomechanically grounded wearable actuation/assistance.
systemAdapted: EMS mapping (upper/lower body) for kinesthetic feedback
Closed-loop skill coaching via wearable sensing/feedback
Assistive walker speed + terrain leveling control
layers.keyImpact: Accurate, wearable force/thermal/tactile feedback and intent-aware assistance for training, teleop, mobility, and XR touch.

layers.name: BEHAVIOR MODELS
layers.goal: Adaptive interaction in driving/AV and in-vehicle XR under cognitive + motion constraints.
signals: Motion sickness + posture stability during driving events
systemAdapted: Profile-State Adaptive in-car touchscreens + time–cost model
In-car VR active locomotion + contextual cues
Synchronized multisensory cues + posture-adaptive VR tasks
layers.keyImpact: Maintains safety/usability/trust in mobility contexts while enabling comfortable passenger XR and clearer AV communication.
